question.text: If Gold spot is Rs 62,000 per 10gm and the 3-month futures are at Rs 63,000, what is the implied annualized cost-of-carry percentage?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Carry = 63,000 - 62,000 = Rs 1,000 for 3 months. Annualized = (1,000/62,000) × (12/3) × 100 = 1.613% × 4 = 6.45% approximately. **The Trap:** 1.61% is the 3-month rate, not annualized. 12% is too high. 4.84% uses 9/12. Always annualize by multiplying the periodic rate by 12/months. **Cross-Exam Context:** Implied cost-of-carry analysis is common across Series VIII and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Comparing implied carry to the prevailing interest rate reveals market expectations — if implied carry > interest rate, storage/insurance costs are being priced in; if lower, convenience yield may be at play.
